data.title: As a developer, the ticket system docs reflect the new bash-native dispatch architecture
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the ticket-system documentation to reflect the new bash-native dispatch architecture. Touch four existing documents: `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` (describe the new library architecture and dispatcher shift from exec to source), `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md` (reflect library-backed dispatch; the public CLI interface is preserved and documented as such), `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ticket-flock-contract.md` (update to describe bash-native `flock(1)` semantics — lock-file path, `flock -x` LOCK_EX, `flock -w 30` timeout, 2-retry loop, cross-path canonicalization via `pwd -P`; any reference to `python3 fcntl.flock` removed or annotated as historical), and `CLAUDE.md` quick reference (document `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` env flag and its sunset plan for the second release cycle). Scan downstream epic specs (170b-2ac9, 736d-b957, 86c8-2d40) for any stale quotes of the flock contract and file follow-up tickets if any are found.

**Why**: Keeping the docs in sync with the new implementation is the knowledge-preservation step. Downstream epics reference the flock contract; a stale description misleads their design work.

**Scope**:
- IN: Updates to `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md`, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md`, `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ticket-flock-contract.md`, and `CLAUDE.md` quick reference; scan of downstream epic specs (170b-2ac9, 736d-b957, 86c8-2d40) for stale flock-contract references with follow-up tickets filed for any found; follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for style.
- OUT: Creating new documentation files or patterns; unrelated doc cleanup; modifying skill files (no prompt or agent changes ship in this epic).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` reflects the new library-backed dispatch architecture (source-style dispatcher, `ticket-lib-api.sh` responsibilities, `_ticketlib_dispatch` helper)
  ← Satisfies: knowledge preservation — not tied to a specific SC
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-cli-reference.md` describes the public CLI interface as preserved and notes that internal dispatch is now library-backed
  ← Satisfies: knowledge preservation
- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ticket-flock-contract.md` describes bash-native `flock(1)` semantics explicitly and removes or annotates any `python3 fcntl.flock` references as historical
  ← Satisfies: knowledge preservation
- When this story is complete, `CLAUDE.md` quick reference documents `DSO_TICKET_LEGACY=1` and its sunset plan
  ← Satisfies: knowledge preservation
- When this story is complete, downstream epic specs (170b-2ac9, 736d-b957, 86c8-2d40) have been scanned for stale flock-contract references and follow-up tickets filed for any found
  ← Satisfies: knowledge preservation

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting, structure, and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
